name: Circular do Planalto Superior — circular route in Serra da Estrela
name_original: Circular do Planalto Superior — percurso circular na Serra da Estrela
region_intro: Hiking around Unhais Da Serra offers exploration of a glacial valley at the foot of the southwestern slopes of Serra da Estrela. The region is characterized by impressive glacial valleys and dramatic granite formations, providing a gateway to Portugal's largest protected natural park. Hikers will find diverse landscapes including high mountains, numerous crystal-clear springs, rivers, and around 25 lagoons. The area's geology showcases glacial morphology with steep walls and moraine deposits. Best hiking trails around Unhais Da Serra The most popular hiking route is Central Massif Route (PR5), a 6.4 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This route offers stunning views of glacial valleys and granite landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Circular do Planalto Superior — circular route in Serra da Estrela, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. This trail explores the high plateau of Serra da Estrela, offering expansive views. Local hikers also love the Covão do Curral Dam – Covão do Ferro Lake loop from Serra da Estrela, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail leading through a landscape featuring the Covão do Curral Dam and Covão do Ferro Lake, often completed in about 1 hour 34 minutes. Hiking around Unhais Da Serra is defined by glacial valleys, granite formations, and high mountain views, with trails following old paths and ancient routes. The network offers options for easy strolls, moderate excursions, and challenging ascents. The routes in Unhais Da Serra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 2,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Unhais Da Serra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Unhais Da Serra?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails around Unhais Da Serra, offering a diverse range of experiences. You'll find options for all skill levels, including 13 easy routes, 25 moderate trails, and 7 more challenging hikes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Unhais Da Serra?
A: Hiking around Unhais Da Serra offers stunning natural landscapes shaped by ancient glaciers. You can expect to see impressive U-shaped glacial valleys, dramatic granite formations, and privileged views of the high mountains of Serra da Estrela, including Torre, the highest point in mainland Portugal. The region also features numerous crystal-clear springs, river beaches, and around 25 lagoons.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Unhais Da Serra?
A: Yes, Unhais Da Serra offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10370014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão do Curral Dam – Covão do Ferro Lake loop from Serra da Estrela&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.2-mile (5.1 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 34 minutes to complete, leading through a scenic landscape featuring the dam and lake.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the area?
A: For experienced trekkers, Unhais Da Serra provides challenging ascents that explore the higher altitudes of Serra da Estrela. While not listed in the guide data, the region research mentions the PR16 CVL – Unhais Canadas Route, which connects Unhais da Serra to the Torre plateau, offering a demanding experience through glacial cirques.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Unhais Da Serra?
A: Yes, many trails in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2175369109/circular-do-planalto-superior-circular-route-in-serra-da-estrela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular do Planalto Superior — circular route in Serra da Estrela&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.5-mile (8.9 km) path that explores the high plateau, offering expansive views.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls or lakes near Unhais Da Serra?
A: Absolutely! The region is rich in water features. You can discover various waterfalls, such as the majestic Vale das Vacas Waterfall and the famous Poço do Inferno. For lakes, the area boasts around 25 lagoons, including Lagoa do Peixão and Covão do Curral Dam – Covão do Ferro Lake. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6019826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hidden cascade at Poço do Inferno&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or viewpoints to see along the trails?
A: Hikers can enjoy numerous viewpoints offering panoramic vistas of the valleys and mountains. Notable examples include Poios Brancos Viewpoint and Miradouro da Varanda dos Carqueijais. You might also encounter unique features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/374228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a fascinating man-made funnel in a lagoon, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2626822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock Passage in the Serra da Estrela Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Unhais Da Serra?
A: The hiking trails around Unhais Da Serra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning glacial valleys, dramatic granite formations, and the diverse range of trails that cater to various fitness levels, from easy strolls to challenging ascents.

Q: Is Unhais Da Serra accessible by public transport for hikers?
A: While Unhais Da Serra is a popular destination, public transport options directly to trailheads can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider private transport for more flexibility in reaching specific starting points for your hikes. For general information on the region, you might find resources on &lt;a href="https://visitcovilha.com/en/trails/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitcovilha.com&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there specific trails that offer great views of the Serra da Estrela mountains?
A: Many trails provide excellent views of the Serra da Estrela mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2132996429/central-massif-route-pr5" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Central Massif Route (PR5)&lt;/a&gt; is particularly known for its stunning views of glacial valleys and granite landscapes, offering a true sense of the region's grandeur. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17313943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Summit, Serra da Estrela – View of Cântaro Gordo loop from Serra da Estrela&lt;/a&gt; provides direct access to the highest point in mainland Portugal with breathtaking panoramic vistas.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Unhais Da Serra?
A: The best season for hiking in Unhais Da Serra generally depends on your preference. Spring (April-June) offers vibrant flora and pleasant temperatures, with lagoons like Lagoa do Peixão coming alive with insect life. Summer (July-August) is warm and sunny, ideal for higher altitude trails. Autumn (September-October) brings beautiful fall colors. Winter (November-March) can be cold with snow at higher elevations, offering a different, more challenging experience for those equipped for it.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the unique geology of the region?
A: Yes, the entire region is a testament to glacial morphology. Trails often traverse impressive glacial valleys, showcase dramatic granite formations, and reveal features like glacial cirques and moraine deposits. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2132996429/central-massif-route-pr5" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Central Massif Route (PR5)&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, offering stunning views of these geological wonders.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Unhais Da Serra?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. In Unhais Da Serra itself, you can often find parking near the village center or specific points of interest. For trails leading into the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, designated parking areas are usually available at popular starting points or viewpoints. It's recommended to check specific route details on komoot for parking suggestions related to your chosen hike.
tips: Beautiful panoramic views from the highest point in Portugal. You can ski here in winter, and there is sometimes snow even in summer.

As the name suggests, this is a large hollow, an amphitheater sculpted by nature, where a lagoon lies that, on hot summer days, can disappear completely. Even so, the landscape remains stunning, imposing and filled with the overwhelming silence of the mountain.
A great landscape. The signage is sometimes very bad. Often just stone men.
